methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $55M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Blackshark.ai is a Series A–stage Defense & Security company founded by Michael Putz that has raised $55M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage private companies (Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M), supplemented by a broader comp universe of eight private firms and two public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $55M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $110M. The peer-median method anchors to the three same-stage, same-sector comparables, which show a median valuation of $300M with a range of $150M–$500M. These two approaches diverge materially, reflecting uncertainty about whether Blackshark.ai's capital efficiency and early traction align with the high end of the peer distribution or represent a more typical progression curve.

The triangulated analysis implies a valuation range of $94M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $205M. The wide spread reflects tension between bottom-up capital-progression modeling and peer-relative positioning. Key uncertainties include the relative quality of Blackshark.ai's technology and customer pipeline versus named comparables, customer concentration risk, and execution risk at scale in the Defense & Security sector. Further due diligence on revenue traction, contract values, and go-to-market efficiency would narrow this range materially.
